name: British Columbia Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA)
summary: British Columbia's private-sector privacy legislation, deemed substantially similar to PIPEDA. It governs how private-sector organizations in BC collect, use, and disclose personal information, including employee personal information. Uniquely among Canadian provinces, BC PIPA also applies to provincial and federal political parties.
keyRequirements: Obtain consent appropriate to the sensitivity of the personal information
Collect personal information only for purposes a reasonable person would consider appropriate
Allow individuals to access and request correction of their personal information
Implement reasonable security measures to protect personal information
Designate a privacy officer responsible for the organization's compliance
Mandatory breach notification to the Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner for BC
Coverage extends to employee personal information and political party data
penalties: Fines up to $100,000 CAD for individuals and $500,000 CAD for organizations per offense. Enforced by the Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner for British Columbia. No private right of action.
